title: DeskModal Catalog Entry
description: Authoritative schema for a single PluginEntry inside marketplace/appmarket/index.json. Authored by F158-W3 (install/update SOTA — delta-first downloads). Aligned with the wire-format served by core-server-api/src/routes/packages/ (Phase 3 G5). The deltas[] array is the W3 contribution — it lets the update-orchestrator (F158-W2) pick the smallest applicable delta package + verify signature + apply without ever re-fetching a full bundle when an incremental path exists. Older clients that don't understand the deltas[] field ignore it transparently (per F158 §18.4 — additive extension; no V2 ladder per quality.md §5).

id.description: Globally unique dot-segmented capability id, e.g. `desk-modal.paper-trading`. Enforced unique by the aggregator (schema.md §Rules #1).

owner.description: GitHub org owning the source repo.

content_type.description: Coarse install-unit classifier. `brand` + `sdk` extend the original schema.md set so .dmbrand + .dmpkg-sdk bundles share the catalog surface (per architecture.md §25 + §18.4.1).

latest_version.description: SemVer of the newest non-pre-release version.

bundle.description: Mirror of `plugin.toml [bundle]` per architecture.md §27 (3-tier metadata). Aggregator copies fields verbatim so the storefront can render tier badges + footprint without re-fetching the manifest.

platforms.description: Per-platform delivery descriptors. Keys MUST be of the form `<os>-<arch>` per the DeskModal platform matrix, or the literal `wasm` cross-platform fallback. `null` value means no asset for that platform.

deltas.description: F158-W3 EXTENSION — array of incremental delta packages keyed by source-version. The update-orchestrator (F158-W2) selects the entry whose `from_version` matches the user's currently-installed version; if none matches it falls back to the full `platforms[*].url` download. Every entry is independently Ed25519-signed under the same `publisher.key_id` as the parent entry — the catalog only carries the digest + size; the raw delta tarball + detached sig live at `delta_url`. The aggregator publishes deltas opportunistically: by default the last N=3 prior versions + always the immediately-previous version (per F158 §8 OC-03). Older clients ignore this field; newer clients consult it first.

from_version.description: SemVer the delta patches FROM (the user's currently-installed version).

to_version.description: SemVer the delta patches TO. MUST equal the entry's `latest_version` (the aggregator validates) — deltas always target the catalog row's published version.

delta_url.description: Absolute URL serving the raw delta bytes. Convention: `/p/<id>/<to_version>/from-<from_version>.dmpkg-delta`. core-server-api Content-Type: `application/x-deskmodal-package-delta`.

delta_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the raw delta bytes (before any HTTP transport encoding).

delta_size_bytes.description: Size in bytes of the delta package. The update-orchestrator picks the smallest applicable delta when N>1 candidates resolve.

full_size_bytes.description: Size in bytes of the full `.dmpkg` this delta reconstructs to. Mirrored from `platforms[*].size_bytes` for client-side bandwidth-saving display ('30% of full download').

format.description: Algorithm + container the bytes are encoded in. `deskmodal-chunk-cas-4kb` is the current zero-dep 4 KB content-addressable chunking scheme implemented in core-server-api/src/store/packages/delta.rs (F156 G5-W2/W4). `bsdiff-brotli` is the queued production-grade upgrade once the bsdiff crate dep lands.

signature.description: Detached signature over the delta bytes. Optional in the catalog entry because the canonical signature is served via the `X-DeskModal-Signature` HTTP header at `delta_url`; clients that prefer in-catalog verification may use this field instead.

prev_artifact_sha256.description: Optional. SHA-256 of the previous-version full artifact this delta was computed against. Lets the client sanity-check it has the right reference bytes BEFORE attempting reconstruction (saves a wasted apply-and-fail cycle when the local `.dmpkg` cache is corrupt).

to_artifact_sha256.description: Optional. SHA-256 of the reconstructed full artifact. Equals the entry's `platforms[<host>].sha256` for the matching platform; carried inline so the client can verify the reconstruction without a separate catalog round-trip.
